title: Digital Equipment Corporation Longitudinal Study, 1985-Present
author: Peter S. Kastner / Perplexity Computer (synthetic archival assembly)
abstract: This longitudinal archival synthesis follows Digital Equipment Corporation from its mid-1980s VAX/VMS enterprise position through DECtp, Alpha, open-systems fragmentation, the 1998 Compaq acquisition, HP/HPE inheritance, and the current OpenVMS/VSI afterlife. It uses the DEC evidence inventory from the Aberdeen archive and explicitly separates Kastner-authored observations from Aberdeen institutional, DEC corporate, and third-party observations.
importance_rationale: DEC is a central enterprise-platform case for understanding minicomputer decline, RISC transitions, OLTP benchmarks, open systems, and acquisition afterlife.
relevance_rationale: The study remains relevant for platform-transition analysis, ecosystem strategy, legacy-system preservation, and archival attribution methodology.
prescience_rationale: The scorecard distinguishes durable technical afterlife from failed independent corporate-platform survival, matching observable successor-chain outcomes.
